column10: review_text

The Al Safwa First Class Lounge was once a model of calm, a true refuge for the weary traveller. In recent times, however, it has become more crowded and less pleasant. The service remains warm and earnest. Al Safwa no longer feels genuinely exclusive; it increasingly resembles just another premium lounge rather than the sanctuary it once was. One is left to wonder whether entrance standards have been relaxed. Access to this lounge is not inexpensive, and I pay those fees willingly for a particular level of experience. I would not hesitate to pay more if it meant restoring the older Al Safwa climate - quiet, restrained, and unmistakably First Class.

Qatar Airways doesn't have a First Class Lounge in Dubai to the same level as Doha. Instead, First Class passengers flying Qatar are invited to the Ahlan First Class Lounge, which offers a calm luxurious atmosphere, with exceptional restaurant-quality food and drinks. Most importantly, I found the staff to be extremely friendly and attentive.

column7: Isn’t pre-boarding for people in wheelchairs?”
column10: When it comes to sheer size, design and facilities, the Qatar Business Class Lounge in Doha has no equal. If you were to describe it with one word it would be – ‘breathtaking’. The attention to detail, quality of furniture and fittings are outstanding. This is matched by the food and beverage offering that includes a full al-a-carte dining experience. Unfortunately, I suffer from terminal cancer and require wheelchair assistance between the lounge and the boarding gates. Despite assurances, this has never gone without a hitch, but this time was the worst. As instructed, I sat in the small waiting area near the lounge entry station. The boarding time printed on my boarding pass came and went without any sign of a wheelchair. I expressed my concern to the staff, only to be told it was too soon, as they were only pre-boarding. My question, “Isn’t pre-boarding for people in wheelchairs?”, received another rebuke. It was only when I demanded to be taken to the aircraft that a wheelchair appeared, still with a member of staff talking insistently in an attempt to justify himself to the point I had to demand he cease talking to me. I was the last passenger to board and very nearly missed the flight.

column7: like going to a 5 star hotel
column10: This lounge is like going to a 5 star hotel with family, quiet and prayers spaces. The quiet places are bedrooms with a shower. One can for a fee soak in a jacuzzi, get massages and mani/pedis. This is a vast lounge with open spaces. Doesn't feel overcrowded even during busy periods. Artifacts from the Museum of Islamic Art are on display. The lounge has its own duty free shop. It has a 250 seat restaurant that serves full service tapas type meals. Staff are welcoming and very professional. For connecting flights, the lounge has its own departure gate which transports passengers in 12 seat buses to their gate. The bathrooms are very clean with constant cleaning by attendants. Since we had a 9 hour layover, we booked one of the bedrooms to rest and shower before our next flight.

column7: one of the best business class lounges
column10: This is huge and spacious lounge on two levels. The lower level has plenty of seating with drinks stations dispersed throughout its length. The upper level has a full restaurant with buffet stations and a full service menu serving tapas sized meals. Having experienced the Al Safwa lounge on our outbound flights, this lounge appeared overcrowded especially on the restaurant level and a bit of let down. The staff is very friendly and welcoming. The only drawback to this lounge is that there was only bathroom on the restaurant level. Nonetheless, this is one of the best business class lounges I've been through.

column7: lounge felt more crowded than usual
column10: Al Safwa First Lounge, Doha I have visited Al Safwa several times, most recently in August 2025. Physically it is an exceptional space, an oasis of calm. What sets it apart from other great lounges in the same league - such as Lufthansa’s First Terminal or the Swiss First Lounge - is its staff, composed largely of Asians (predominantly Indians), whose warmth gives the place a character unique to the region. By contrast, the European lounges, while elegant and luxurious, can feel somewhat sterile. It was therefore with some dismay that my latest visit fell short of past standards. The lounge felt more crowded than usual, and the wait for a shower exceeded an hour. My sense is that the entry requirements have been relaxed. I would urge Qatar Airways to preserve the exclusivity that once defined Al Safwa.
Outstanding Business Class lounge at Doha Airport offering different dining options, comfortable seating, and shower facilities. Highly recommend the facilities.

This has to be the best business class lounge in the world. It is huge, on two levels, offer many food and drink options, including a la carte. The lounge is very comfortable and There are also showers, luggage storage and plenty of staff members to assist you in any way. i needed assistance to get to a gate far from the lounge, I could not have been more pleased with the service, much appreciated!
Excellent lounge. The best for a long time ago. Perfect service, good food, great comfort, sunny atmosphere at 11 floor. Excellent staff - warm welcome. Congrats !!!

First time visiting this beautiful lounge and definitely not the last. I decided to check out this lounge after previously visiting the same one in the South Pier of the airport. Compared to the South Pier lounge, the Platinum and Gold Lounge North offers a more spacious and open ambiance, providing plenty of room to relax before your next connecting flight. The food selection was excellent, offering both à la carte and buffet options. The staff were attentive and had a great sense of humor, which made the experience even more enjoyable. The only downside? The long queue for the shower rooms. But overall, it was a great experience!

The Qatar Airways lounge for Platinum and Gold members at Concourse C is a perfect blend of luxury and relaxation. The space is beautifully designed—spacious and comfortable, offering an ideal atmosphere to unwind before a flight. Every detail feels thoughtful, from the comfortable seating to the variety of quiet corners. The hospitality is exceptional; the staff are attentive, elegant, and always ready to assist, making you feel truly valued as a member. Whether you're enjoying a meal, catching up on work, or simply relaxing, the lounge experience is peaceful and seamless. It’s a fantastic retreat that elevates the entire travel experience.
column11: Platinum and Gold Lounge Concourse C

column7: find it one of the best lounges
column10: Nicely located lounge close to Gate B2 with friendly staff at reception and plenty of space for guests. Good variety is limited but I personally find it one of the best lounges in HIA with excellent WiFi and clean toilets.

Incredible eating options, with excellent bar services. Service generally outstanding, friendly and helpful. I wish I would always have this standard when travelling business class, also with other airlines. It comes close to what a real first class lounge should be, even if travelling in business class. Not airline or lounge to be blamed, unpleasant are some co-travelers dressed like going to the beach or coming from the gym. I am afraid that is a general trend while travelling long haul, but I think it is a little disrespectful towards the well groomed and impeccable staff.

This the best lounge at Dhaka International Airport. Very well decorated lounge that has a very nice prayer room for Muslims and clean toilet. It serves Indian, continental and Thai-Chinese food; food was delicious. The beverages include fresh juice, soft drinks and tea-coffee. The food was prepared by the Westin Hotel, Dhaka. There is a small library to read books, magazines and newspapers.

This lounge is absolutely the best. Customer service is outstanding. As I was walking gingerly with a full cup of macchiato coffee to my seat, a service came right over and offered to take the coffee to my seat! He even made an extra coffee because I spilled some from the cup onto the saucer. The other agents kept track of my connection flight times and reminded me to proceed to the gate. The only suggestion I have is that the rest rooms could have been larger with shower amenities.

A visit to the recently refurbished Al-Safwa lounge at Doha's Hamad airport is always an extraordinary experience. Admission is only for passengers travelling in Qatar Airways First Class or QR Platinum members. Status customers from other Oneworld partners have no access. There is an escalator to the spacious reception where people need to go to be admitted. There seem to be more staff than guests and the atmosphere is friendly and warm. People can go enjoy a drink at the bar or have a meal at the à la carte restaurant. Service at the restaurant needs to be streamlined. I think the staff have not been properly trained as waiters. I hadn't finished my starter yet when soup and main course arrived. The selection of food is great and all I ordered (lentil soup, wagyu beef carpaccio and lamb) was delicious. The champagne was Lanson Brut Black Label. After dinner, I went to the spa and had a great massage. It needs to be mentioned that the spa treatments are not included and have to be paid for. Altogether a fantastic experience difficult to be topped.

Beautifully appointed lounge with plenty of quiet and relaxed seating area. Very nice restaurant area and small but very adequate food range. Very nice toilet and shower facilities . Attentive service. Very clean.

Nice lounge. I arrived around 8am for my 11am Malaysia Airlines flight to KL. I headed straight to bathrooms who had attendants waiting outside to service regularly. I sat in lounge area which had ample charging facilities and a nice view of some departure gates. I went to dining area which had a sumptuous display of cold items available. The buffet hot food was a little tepid in temperature but tasty enough. Service was efficient without being over bearing. I was advised that non Qatar flights were not announced so you have to check the screen yourself for boarding updates. The lounge was very clean and well presented with drinks and snacks available to help yourself to. Very pleasant experience.

This is a fabulous lounge. Service is impeccable. Beautiful view of the Garden area of the airport. The staff are friendly and attentive. They try to anticipate what the guest wants. Any reasonable wish will be fulfilled. The selection of food is variable and large. One can order à la carte or use the buffet. There is even a sushi bar. Premium drinks are readily available. There is a quiet area with several tastefully decorated resting rooms which operates on a “first come, first served” base. Drinks cannot be taken into the rooms though. The lounge is exceptionally clean and well kept, but it only opened a few weeks ago. There is a Louis Vuitton cafe which, unfortunately has not opened yet. This is one of my favorite lounges and the best business lounge I have been to lately. Certainly better than First Class lounges of most airlines. Can’t wait to use it on my next flight via Hamad International.

column7: staff was very lovely and helpful
column10: Very nice and big lounge. During my stay there was only a handful other people at the lounge which made it a very nice experience. Also the staff was very lovely and helpful. Food was buffet style but of good quality. The wine on the other hand was surprisingly bad.

column7: The shower wait was one hour
column10: While three people scanned my boarding pass to access the lounge and they all noted that there was a problem with the boarding pass, no one could tell me what to do or where to go - or what time I needed to leave the lounge to arrive at the gate in time. Very large and airy lounge with lots of seating and food and drink areas. I couldn't access the menu though was my boarding pass didn't have the required QR code. The shower wait was one hour which seemed like too long. Luckily at the very chaotic gate I received the new and correct boarding pass.

column7: good with a la carte dining
column10: Lounge in Bangkok is small, but good with a la carte dining. A single champagne, Gosset, was offered. Lounge was almost full and quite vibrant. One of the three best lounges in BKK.

column7: No fish, terraria, no music, no gym
column10: El Safwa is too huge with little load. No fish, terraria, no music, no gym. Worse for me is the design and the granite, glass, and steely. Not a single cozy place. The dorms are OK, very small; lack windows. The high ceilings allow for fresh air. The a la carte dining offered is supposed to be an asset, however my waiting time is >30 Minutes. There is no buffet in the lounge.
Excellent service and well managed lounge with comfortable seating. The overall cleanliness and attention to detail is hard to beat. Quality of the food and wines were terrific.

column7: Food and drinks were pretty well sorted
column10: A lounge with 2 sections. We were guided to the premium section where we were for approx. 2 ½ hours. The lounge is decorated in an attractive way and very comfortable. Unfortunately almost no daylight and a little dark. View on the departure hall. We can imagine that when crowded, people feel slightly unpleasant, perhaps claustrophobic. Great internet. We did not notice if shower facilities were available. The lounge was clean. Food and drinks were pretty well sorted, varied and of good quality and above all tasty. Staff was nice.

column7: they switched to the Marhaba Lounge
column10: Before covid, Qatar Airways used the Qantas Lounge. Apparently they switched to the Marhaba Lounge. Unfortunately and bad choice. The lounge is small, and the sound of the TV filled the room in a very unpleasant way. Although no one was watching, the attendant refused to switch off the sound. We seriously considered to go to another lounge and pay a fee for entrance. The lounge consists of 3 rooms: a bar/buffet with very unattractive furniture, a room to work, however very dark and the main room with the TV noise. Shower facilities were very poor: small space, no vent. Food and drinks limited but OK. Nice view on the tarmac.

The Premiere Lounge is OK but nothing more. WiFi password on a sign at the reception counter. Voucher is not enough to get in so keep your boarding card ready. Alcohol behind the bar so you have to order it. Coffee maker broken so no coffee with milk was available.
This first time I came to the Mariner Lounge since I have been seafarer. It was really best experience, Sohan from f&b was very humble and friendly. Thanks a lot, and all here are free of charge

Qatar Airways use the Menzies Executive Lounge at Stockholm Arlanda Airport, and it is the worst lounge I ever visited! Boring, small and dirty. Extremely limited drinks and food. Stale bread, tasteless cheese, bad red wine on tap, tasteless beer on tap. When you finally got a glass, it was warm. I'll never go back to this lounge. I rather pay for a good tasty sandwich and beer in the terminal. I don't understand how a prestigious airline as Qatar Airways can use this lounge.

This lounge is world famous for its size, design and great services. I had not used it for several years during the hiatus of the pandemic. But on the last trip, transiting through DOH, it was a chance to re-acquaint ourselves. The main priority was to use the showers. But there was a queue of about 10 people waiting to use the only 2 working showers in the larger of the two shower facilities. The other shower facility also had a long queue, with no Qatar staff managing its usage. The lounge itself was extremely busy, with very little seating availability. For their flagship lounge, Qatar Airways seem to have greatly under planned the demand, or over-offered its availability. Luckily there was a very helpful member of staff who helped take us to the Gold Lounge and to use the facilities there, so we managed showers, and to catch our connecting flight. That is impressive, but if Qatar Airways wants to retain its premier title, it needs to rethink its strategy here. And quickly, with the upcoming FIFA tournament.

I used this lounge when it first opened about 4 years ago, and it was excellent. Had read that it was not maintained properly, but on the recent departure from Bangkok, everything was in good working order, with excellent service from staff, and good snacks, The only quibble was the champagne: only Pommery, which was corked. They opened another, but it too was corked. There was quite a wide selection of hot and cold food, and given the dire food service inflight, it would have been wiser to choose to eat here, especially as we complied with the airlines recommended 3 hour pre-flight check in, (which took about 30 minutes, from check in to lounge).

The Al-Mourjan lounge in Hamad International Airport. As with any lounge experience the second time round is often better than the first. I had specifically chosen to have a long layover in Doha as, due to my age, I find that two shorter flights with a layover between is a much more comfortable experience. My first visit to the lounge was a seven hour layover between flights. I didn't know of the quiet area and that I could have a sleep for awhile so I didn't take advantage of that on my first visit. However I did use the shower facilities which were absolutely superb. It will be difficult to find anything bad to say about the staff in the lounge is they were attentive clearing used plates and cups not intruding when it was clear that they were not needed. The lounge is magnificent it's been awarded a place as the number one business class lounge in the world and I can easily understand why. I was there during one of the busy periods when I arrived in Doha, and even so there was plenty more seating available food was available links for available and the number of staff employed were easily enough for the guests in the lounge. We now come along to the food in the restaurant. The breakfast was very nice I had eggs Florentine they were beautifully presented wonderfully served with excellent table service, I had arrived at 7 o'clock in the morning and the lunchtime service didn't take place until 11 o'clock in the morning, had I known then what I know now, I would have taken the opportunity to go and have a nap in the quiet area. However since my flight to London was a little after 12 o'clock I didn't want to miss the chance of lunch in the restaurant so stayed awake. Once again in the restaurant presentation was beautiful service was excellent unfortunately the quality of the food that was served was a huge disappointment, the two options which I considered to be and salmon. The salmon although the presentation on the plate was absolutely beautiful was crunchy on the outside and dry as a bone on the inside. I did try the beef and was told that medium rare was not available as it is prepared by a catering company outside the airport and the restaurant reheat and serves it therefore it has to be well done. Some people may like well done beef, it certainly is not to my taste. To the credit of the restaurant staff I did tell them that the salmon was really rather overcooked, which was an understatement, and they insisted on preparing another piece of salmon for me to try, the chef who cooked it came to the table, I was too embarrassed to tell them that it was virtually no different from the first piece crunchy on the outside, which could be out an okay thing, but still dry as a bone on the inside, so I politely declined to tell them my impression of the second piece. I didn't have time to try anything else on the menu as my flight was being called.

column7: using the Miracle lounge in Bangkok
column10: Qatar Airways has closed their own lounge in Bangkok airport during the pandemic, I'm not sure when it's going to reopen. As a result they using the Miracle lounge in Bangkok airport. I would say that the Miracle lounge is only really so-so, the drink selection is quite limited as is the food selection. Whilst of course as in any business Lounge you can eat as much as you like and drink as much as you like the selection really dissuaded you from doing that. The lounge is okay, you do have a multitude of power points so it's not difficult to find a seat with a power point adjacent to it,. Of course you can eat as much as you like I found that a curry and rice was the best option available to my taste, there were few other snacks that I tried but nothing really exciting. During my 4 flights I tried three different lounges one in Bangkok one in Doha, twice. and one in Heathrow Airport the Bangkok Lounge was definitely at the bottom of the list. It's definitely better than waiting in the main terminal or at the gate with no question as to that, I sincerely hope that Qatar Airways is able to reopen their own lounge in the near future.

Since the upgrades to the terminal Qatar Airways now use the 1903 lounge and it is is bright, airy and well laid out with lots of comfortable seating areas and a very good choice of food and drinks available. We were there at 5am and were welcomed by a very friendly member of staff who handed us over to Alexander and Janine who looked after us superbly. I would thoroughly recommend this lounge to any traveller using T2 it makes a wonderful start to your journey.
Very clean lounge with both a buffet for food as the possibility to order freshly prepared food. Impressive selection of beverages. Friendly service and very clean restrooms

Probably the most luxurious lounge I ever saw, and feeling very sorry that it was so early in the morning so i could not try some of the great wines available including 2 second growth Bordeaux. I had a great breakfast with delicious fruits and could not resist one glass of premium Champagne. Extremely friendly service and luxurious and clean restrooms

column7: Coral Lounge in Phuket must be the worst
column10: The Coral Lounge in Phuket operated by AOT must be the worst and most depressing business lounge ever. Super small with furniture from the 1980s. No champagne, ice cold red wine and seating like a school dinner hall. A few armchairs are there but since the lounge is so small you are lucky if you get one. Though you can have a neck and shoulder massage while you drink your cold red wine. This is not where you want to spend 1 or 2 hours waiting!!

I stayed in the Mariners Lounge during my transit stay at Doha airport. The lounge is very clean. Frequent sanitation being carried out by the staff. Food choices are adequate and always replenished. The toilet is large enough to provide the essentials including a shower room. Wifi connection is superb. Most of all, the staff are very accommodating and that’s what made my stay worthwhile considering the long hours spent before my next flight. Thank you for the efficient service.

column7: best business class lounge in the world
column10: This lounge has many spots where you can pick up sparkling water and espressos, in addition to full menu dining and buffets which are manned by servers. I had a delightful shower and a nice rest in a quiet zone. We had a close to 8 hour layover, so we made use of the amenities throughout the lounge. Easily the best business class lounge in the world!

Spacious, quiet, peaceful place that allows for good distribution of space between other lounge users. Good range of facilities. Very polite, helpful and friendly staff. Good range of food and beverage.

column7: stay at lounge was sensational and tremendous
column10: I had a great experience at the Oryx/Al-Maha lounge. Staff was very friendly and helping. The Lounge ambience and cleanliness were remarkable. I had never observed such a excellent level of cleanliness in rest room with outstanding shower facility decorated with world class toiletries. Just one thing to improve is the range of food, which was very limited but what was available, was good quality in both taste and selection of ingredients. Overall my stay at lounge was sensational and tremendous.

I would like to thank the staff in their arrivals lounge in Doha, for their hospitality and extremely caring. Mr Kunwar was especially kind to me and made sure that every single detail was taken care of.
The Aspire Lounge they use at Gatwick has a poor food selection. The whole place is in need of refurbishment, tables were not cleaned.

This first class lounge is nothing short of exceptional and I can understand why it is the worlds best. The interior decor, finishes and features are above most 5 star hotels I've visited, and although I only had about 90 mins stay, I was able to enjoy a fabulous breakfast that was freshly cooked for me, and served by the friendliest staff. The bathrooms were exceptional, and so many other features I did not use (bedrooms, spa etc). Other staff on the entrance counter and inside the lounge were also exemplary.

This is not the Qatar Airways business class lounge but is for Oneworld Emerald and Sapphire FFs. Cheap seats, not enough capacity during peak hours, limited F&B options, and an overall vibe of indifference and inattention.

I went first time in this Lounge which is at Hamad Doha Airport. I was travelling in economy class but i got upgraded to Silver Member of Qatar Airways Privilege Club and got access to Business Class Lounge. It was very nice and huge with wide variety of Food and sweets and drinks staff was very supportive and respectful. I really enjoyed this Lounge.

This lounge in the early morning is mayhem. It is difficult to find seats at the restaurant. The food is decent however and the staff are friendly. Drinks order are taken a few minutes after you get a seat. The lounge can be improved with more seating.

Visited this lounge during a stopover. Spacious and clean but lacks character. The staff were disappointing and gave an air of superiority over the traveller. I would recommend the excellent restaurants in the airport where the food is superior with a better atmosphere.

This lounge does not advertise it nor does it show up on the Oneworld lounge finder. Yet it is open and there are signs inside the airport that point to the lounge. The lounge is almost identical to their lounge in Paris. There were comfortable seats and when I ordered something at the bar, the bartender said "I'll serve it at your seat." The food there was just immaculate. There was a hot buffet filled with mostly Thai and Arabic dishes, and actual dining tables with utensils already set up! The staff would always apologise if they ran out of something and would fill your plate up for you on request. They ask you want drink you want and if you want it refilled. My parents had a full meal so they could maximise sleep on the flight. The choice of beverages is astounding, their choice of champagne was Bollinger! The best bit about this lounge has to be the cakes. Chocolate opera cake with gold flakes, and the best Baklava in Bangkok! The other parts of the lounge were like a super comfortable waiting room, and you felt like royalty while you were in any part of this lounge. I understand why they don't call it a business class lounge. Simply because it would make all other business class lounges look like glorified waiting rooms. One thing that is also very cheeky is the fact that the lounge is located next door to Singapore Airlines, and having been in that lounge, it simply does not compare to Qatar Airways offering. Too bad it's only open for business class passengers, not Oneworld Sapphire/Emerald but at least you can access this lounge using any Oneworld business class ticket.

This First Class Lounge is for OneWorld members travelling in economy class. It's a small L shaped room that has limited seating and disgraceful food options. I wouldn't have booked Qatar on this trip if I knew that this member's lounge was this bad, as the lounge quality is an important driver to airline decision. Wouldn't recommend the lounge to any oneWorld Elites hoping for a first class experience.

Al Mourjan lounge is a great lounge which tends to be crowded especially in the morning busy hours. I would like to focus upon the quality of lounge food which seems to be rather declining. The veg Korma was watery and full of cream, so was the tandoori marinated fish. My question is why can't food be prepared in this lounge to ensure freshness and quality.

column7: spacious but it is also crowded
column10: Using the Al Mourjan Lounge from 0030hrs 3rd November to 0700hrs. The lounge is impressively huge and spacious with lots of seating spaces of various styles. Few sleeping couches are available at the back area of the lounge. However, due to its brightness and noise from the planes in nearby gates, I'm unable to get any sleep. Better sound-proof walls / glasses and less bright sections are required in my opinion. Quiet period in the lounge is between 0230 till 0500 hours (Qatar time) and i'm lucky to be able to use the toilet and shower room at 0400 hrs. The toilet and shower rooms are clean and consistently monitored by the staff. However at 0600 hr, there is a long queue for the toilet and shower rooms and overheard a passenger saying that he has been waiting for 40 minutes for the shower room. Main dining room on the upper floor is also spacious but at 0600hr it is also crowded. As Qatar Airways is targeting business class passengers as their main client, the impressively vast Al Mourjan seems to be inadequate to accommodate all. Separation of business class passengers into several lounges might be required. Having another lounge near Gate D, E (served by a train ride from main terminal) might be helpful.

column7: one of the nicest I have visited
column10: This lounge is one of the nicest I have visited, impeccable staff serving great food and drinks, l have used the Emirates lounge at Heathrow , and although very good, l find this lounge more personal. I will make another visit on an upcoming trip, and look forward to my visit, the freshly cooked breakfasts are great, as is the Champagne, cant wait.

The third party lounge Qatar Airways use here is located at the 2B non-schengen terminal of the airport. After the passport control and the duty free corridor turn left and go upstairs. Signs are not really clear so people may struggle finding it. The lounge is quite small compared to other European lounges and may be busy in peak hours. As you enter, the buffet is on the right, seating in front and the other side. I visited the lounge in the afternoon, the meal options were basic, without warm meal. The soda dispenser was out of order so only unchilled soda cans were provided at the time. There are no toilets inside, you must visit the public toilets outside. Since the lounge is located on top floor of the terminal, at the new wing, it has an excellent apron view.

Qatar Airways use the airport Premium Lounge. Quite small lounge but they have good selection of food and beverages. No smoking area. Free Wi-Fi. Staff helpful and friendly. Be sure to check your departure gate and check with the staff how long it will take you to walk there!

Huge lounge! But can get busy especially in the morning. 2 restaurants open 24/7. Bar and tabele service if you catch a waiter. Several coffee stations and soft drinks. Clean restrooms and showers. Small room for smoking. Not the most comfortable chairs if you have a longer transfer.

Really don't understand how Qatar airways can pay to use this handling lounge. Last time they served vegetarian lasagna and a salad. Champagne was out and took the staff about 30 minutes to get hold of a bottle. Generally a bad lounge.
Lounge overlooking the runway and gates. Clean and comfortable with a good selection of food and drinks. Nice helpful staff. Free Wi-Fi password on the tables.

I was booked on one of the first flights from Cardiff to Doha and have flown with QR many times. Qatar Airways have to use the Cardiff Airport Lounge which is hidden away but reasonably signposted. On entry, my heart sank as I am used to the QR business lounge at LHR. It's dismal and an awful start to my holiday experience. Poor drinks selection, poor food choice, down at heel and awful. Seating was poor, tables bland, atmosphere nil. Tables were left uncleared In the lounge, I was asked for my boarding pass, and then instructed to go through another door - my spirits soared! Behind these doors more of the same, 1980's school canteen theme. A dump. QR- please get your stuff together and rectify this shambles.

The Al Maha lounge in Doha is situated on the arrival floor, after security and passport control. Usually accessible for Al Maha customers but not for business and first class travellers. I was lucky to be redirected there due to the refurbishment of the business class arrival lounge. The lounge has excellent toilet and shower facilities, nice dining area and even a business centre. Upon my early morning arrival snacks and warm breakfast options were served with coffee, tea, orange juice and sodas. The place was almost empty so the staff had plenty of time and capacity to take care of us. Altogether it is a great place to refresh after a long flight, but as I mentioned, it is usually reserved for Al Maha customers.

column7: Had to wait for a shower
column10: This lounge is average for an airline that markets itself as top tier. Had to wait 40 mins for a shower and the reception staff are not particularly helpful. The food I had in the restaurant was not high quality.

Nice and cozy Oslo handling lounge above the departure floor in the international part of the airport. It opens at 5:30, alcohol is served"only from 6:30. I visited early in the morning when only cold buffet breakfast was provided. The range was a bit limited, but enough for a light meal. There are comfy chairs and sofas around with sockets at some of them, but it can get crowded during peak hours due to its size. The WiFi connection was quick and stable. Nice lounge without any extravaganza or show stopper feature.

The huge lounge is situated above the departures floor. There is a spacious area on the lower floor with lots of armchairs and some coffee stations. At the back, there is a buffet area, quiet zone and showers. I tried to sleep in the quiet zone, but the shape of those sitting whatevers made it impossible. On the upper floor there is a restaurant, with great selection of cold arabic humus and salads. The warm buffet provides a great selection of meat and vegetarian meals. There is a great soft and alcoholic drink selection.

The Al Maha transit lounge at Hamad Airport in Doha is small, but it works twenty-four hours and is always clean and organized. The bathrooms are clean. The quality of the food is average and there are non-alcoholic drinks available. The only bad thing is we do not have comfortable chairs to relax in long waits and to get some alcoholic drink we have to ask the waiters.

Excellent, spacious and well-appointed lounge. Easy to reach from arrivals, signage to lounge clear, access the lounge from the main transit terminal. Take escalator to lounge, a QA attendant will guide you and verify your ticket and lounge pass. Interior of lounge is two floors, buffet restaurant and bar is on 2nd floor. First floor has many rest/waiting areas and facilities include: showers, sleep areas, quiet area, prayer room, business center with free printing, 1st floor breakfast/sandwich restaurant, fast WiFi service. Lounge is surprisingly quiet despite its size. Tends to have many visitors from 6:30 am onwards.

We have visited both the business and first class lounges at Doha Airport and they are some of the best lounges we have seen in our travels so far. Incredible service, hundreds of employees are there at every step to address all concerns. The business class lounge was massive, had separate nap areas, quiet areas for working, huge eating area upstairs with great selection of food and drinks. The architecture in the lounge is beautiful, there is a massive waterfall fixture in the center of the lounge facility. The customer service during our breakfast was better than a lot of upscale restaurants we have to been to in the US, absolutely killer job by the lounge team

Unfortunately this airport has 2 shared business lounges that are used by around 15 airlines so depending on the time of day you fly the space can feel as crowded as the gate. On this day we were lucky enough to be assigned to a secondary lounge or an overflow lounge that was only being used by our airline and I think 1 more. This lounge had no bathroom so you had to leave and go to the crowded one to use the facilities if you needed. Manila business lounges need a serious make over, both lounges looked, felt, and smelled old. The glass is covered in humidity and the view is horrendous. The service I also have to say is robotic. They all say “Sir and Mam” so excessively that is just not genuine and it makes you even feel uncomfortable at times. The answer is usually “no” followed by a fake smile and the “Sir or Mam” at the end. Manila service in the past was warm and genuine it’s really robotic now and the general attitude seems to be that no one really cares but in a friendly and polite way. The food was mediocre and nothing to brag about however the drink selection was decent. Plenty of soft and alcoholic choices to chose from. I think with a little bit of work, effort, direct supervision and training this can be a great lounge because the destination is amazing. Too bad the experience leaving was dull.

column7: showers is what I enjoyed the most
column10: This lounge has plenty of rooms, quiet rooms, family rooms, Internet rooms, and showers. The showers is what I enjoyed the most because after a 10 hour flight and then a 14 hour flight that shower came in very handy. Unfortunately the food is mediocre and I was not able to try the WiFi due to the short time I had before my next flight. This lounge is not only for business class passengers but you can also pay to use.

This is a new lounge that proves CDG airports curves can be wonderful. Modeled similar to the London lounge (missing the Lindt chocolate bowls) but awesome. Showers don’t have toilets but honestly who cares. Staff wonderful, food great - a fantastic new lounge for a great airline. My only query - no chocolate ice cream - but again, I’m not complaining.

column7: falls short of what I need
column10: This lounge has been appalling on the past 2 visits. Ground staff are barely knowledgeable about One World status levels nor what those levels are meant to offer. Al Mourjan lounge looks grand and oozes promise but it falls short of what I need. Food is western or Asian. Or a sandwich. Thai beef salad? Pasta was dry and insipid. Nothing is a la carte. Power access to charge phones in the restaurant they have 1 table with power access and that requires a 2m cable to connect. I write this on the edge of a seat with a 2m cable stretching around the corner. And watch out if you are One World Sapphire or Emerald. Twice now - and I am ticketed C on QR the ground staff have tried to send me off to their First Class lounge. The staff in the lounge are affable, well-presented and efficient.

column7: one of the best I've ever been
column10: Due to short transit times on my flights only had short visit to business lounge on return trip, it's one of the best I've ever been. On previous flights I've had 8 hours in transit no a problem when you have this wonderful lounge with really excellent staff. A fine choice of food either full course or just a snack.

The Qatar Airways Business lounge at Heathrow is first rate. Probably the best I have been to. Relaxed with an option of buffet or restaurant dinning, and the food is pretty respectable. Food you want to eat with great service, table clothes and friendly approachable staff. Generally airlines have their best lounges on their home territory, this is not the case with Qatar's Doha lounge. It is is large and looks impressive but it's rather impersonal. Signage is poor, the food very average - it is perhaps the weak link in what is a very good airline. Scores below are for Doha, London would get top scores!

Having spent more than five layovers in last two years, I can say a few things about this place. Lounge is quiet, easy to get to and provides good service. Bar is well stocked, there is a quiet area where you can lay down. Food quality is good, they do have good showers and rest rooms. There is enough privacy, great place to store luggage. Internet works.

The lounge is superb. I’m not sure if it’s the wonderful staff, the variety of well presented food, the ambience or the free chocolates by the fistful down near the kitchen. It’s lovely. A long shower in a large clean bathroom, towels that don’t smell like they fell out of a one star hotel - this is it. Seriously lovely, small enough to be intimate, a joy to visit.

Excellent experience. The lounge is wonderful, relaxing to the eye and incredibly comfortable. The setup is not to packed, as many other lounges, so a lot of space can be enjoyed. Food is excellent, probably the best I encountered so far. Connectivity is good, crew is supportive as expected.

This is a fantastic lounge. Great opportunity to have a shower and something to drink between flights. This is so different to lounges in europe or the US. Plenty of staff. The showers were clean and looked like spa showers. They have bar and beverage secions and a restaurant. Just awesome.

Big lounge with a variety of seating areas etc. The food on offer is fresh, although depending on the time limited. The quantity of shower facilities is very limited and even outside rush hours one has to wait. Their is something like a booking list but it did not work, the best one just wait at the reception and one might be lucky. The lounge still does not has the air conditioning of the shower suites under control: one showers and is wet again after the shower because it is hot and humid like a steam room.

column7: vast with a very open feel
column10: ❎ Unverified | We had a 4 hour stopover flying from Manchester UK to Sydney Australia and spent some time in the lounge. This lounge is vast with a very open feel, chairs and tables are placed well apart, and there seemed to be a number of workstations available with what looked like tablets set up at each station. There were at least two areas for eating, one for light meals such as sandwiches, the other for cooked meals. We had a very nice sandwich each which was served warm. There were also a number of coffee/tea stations available with staff there to assist.

Nice enough lounge, big (vast) and very elegant. It's starting to date. The chairs in the restaurant are getting a bit haggard. The food is good, but the staff seemed preoccupied with each other. I seated myself in the upstairs lounge, no one approached at the tables restraunt section. I'm not exactly sure what to make of this. Not normal service at all.

Qatar Airways Al Safwa First Lounge at Hamad Airport. Museum of Islamic art architecture inspired with artifices on display. Dimensioned for the gods. A fountain from the sky, unburied, exquisite, unique. Service and food choice exceptional. No barbers shop though - I like it for my grey old beard. This seriously redefines a lounge, it's more like a destination inside some classy space ship. A new type of lounge, unique.

A quiet but very basic third party lounge (Skyserv Lounge) compared to others around the world. On the positives it is very clean and tidy. Negatives are that the food selection is extremely limited almost non existed (only a couple of brioche sandwiches) and there are no power sockets to charge electronic devices. All in all very basic

I visited the Araliya handling agent lounge which Qatar Airways use at Colombo Airport. The lounge is basic, tightly packed worn out seating, slow wifi. Cold unenidble food. Very limited food options, no local food. The lounge is so brightly lit it's as good as a tanning booth, couldn't find any recharging stations only single wall sockets requiring adaptor. Not a premium lounge by any means.

column7: one of the best lounges visited
column10: Qatar Airways lounge at Heathrow Airport is a top notch lounge and one of the best lounges I have ever visited. Staff were friendly and courteous, food was excellent, heaps of seating with fast Wi-Fi. As soon as entered the lounge and sat down a member of staff came up to me and asked me if I would like a drink. Service at its best. Passengers were well informed by staff of the short delay to the flight. Other airlines with lounges at Heathrow should take note of the high level of service and quality of food provided in this lounge.

I wrote last month that I was underwhelmed by this lounge, as I found it to be too big and impersonal, lacking any snug areas to hide away in. But on my latest, slightly longer, transit through DOH, I was able to discover the 'quiet area' which has showers next to it and a restaurant/bar just outside. This was just what I wanted. The quiet area is exceptionally peaceful; you have a private room for your party, to relax on loungers in peace (though their metal arms could be made more comfortable). The shower rooms were clean and well maintained, though it would have been nice to have a shoe horn available

A well designed, clean, organised lounge with ample seating and great staff. Play area for children with MBC 3 (Children's channel) was an added plus. Also within the lounge is a small area to buy perfumes. This lounge offers bedrooms based on an hourly rate. Cannot fault it. Overall nice experience and look forward to visiting it again.

column7: Al Maha Lounge which is bigger
column10: Was supposed to visit the Al Maha Lounge as per Lounge Key (Bank Credit Card Privilege), however was told by staff it is full, and I was directed to the First Class Lounge on the other side. Was disappointed as it was overcrowded, noisy and food below standard for a first class lounge and the queues to get food were long. Never visiting this lounge again and will insist on the Al Maha Lounge which is bigger and more organised.

I do not rave about this lounge because it's hard to find because signage is atrocious and the lounge is just too big. It may be visually impressive but it lacks warmth, charm or comfortable places to wait for your flight. Restaurant buffet is nowhere near as good as food used to be, even in the old airport.

Very beautiful and functional lounge. Nice and luxurious also. Two level lounge with plenty of couches and coffee bar at the ground. On the upper floor there is a restaurant, a full bar and a coffee place. Flawless service, great food, nice showers and toiltes.

I had a seven hour layover in this lounge and the scale, architecture and styling of this incredible building doesn't disappoint. All soaring space and blonde marble, the lounge is more of a 5 star hotel in both concept and size. While there were large numbers of staff ready to serve weary travellers, the service, while eager and genuine, doesn't always deliver. Arriving at 1am, I was unable to get a bed in the sleep rooms, being told that the next available bed wouldn't be until 7am. I was shown to the 'family area' which featured large sectioned semi private spaces with subdued lighting, comfortable sofas, armchairs and TVs. Before deciding to sleep on the sofa, I asked for a pillow and blanket which never appeared. In the morning, the showers were occupied with 8 people on the waiting list before me. I managed to find a single shower in the family area and after a wait of 20 mins, was able to use that. Again, nice to be able to shower but not if the designers of the lounge had underestimated capacity and the increased traffic for beds at night time and showers first thing in the morning. Food was good, cappuccino was from a machine and the italian section of the heavily promoted a la carte menu featured 3 different types of pasta.

This handing lounge is the only lounge for business class passengers in the international section of Kolkata Airport, used by all airlines. I had an hour or so before the flight and used the lounge. It is a basic facility with everything you need - food (Indian), snacks, coffee and tea, a selection of alcoholic drinks, free wi-fi, local newspapers, few trade magazines, comfortable couches, a couple of TV screens, and a board listing departing flights. There is one desk top computer which was used the entire time I was there by lounge staff. It is a manned bar but the bartender was not present when I walked up to it. He arrived shortly. The bathroom was clean but the fixtures looked cheap, dated, and crude. The presentation of the food is not appealing and the dining section is dark. At a small cooking station, pasta dishes could be ordered. The lounge serves its purpose but the layout and furniture is such you cannot meaningfully do any real work other than checking emails on your own device.

As an Executive Platinum/Emerald status flyer, Qatar Airways invited me to relax at the American Airlines Admiral Lounge at New York JFK just across the gate from the flight would depart. The lounge is located behind another lounge. There were plenty of drinks, buffet food, snacks, free wi-fi, desk top computers, couches with charging stations, newspapers and magazines. It was crowded but quiet. The two hours I had before my flight started to board was well spent. A staff came by announcing that the Qatar Airways flight was ready to go. I did not use the bathroom or the showers. All in all, a good place to wait.

The Plaza Premium lounges used in Phnom Penh Qatar Airways QR is a 3rd party lounges but of excellent quality. It is new, outstanding, positive surprise with all facilities (incl. fitness room!) and local, hot foods. 4 wines, 2 draft beers, no champagne. Glass panorama windows to 2 sides. Fast internet, lots of support staff. Roomy with good air climate.

column7: lounge at Oslo is pretty average
column10: As a Qatar Airways business lounge this handling agent lounge at Oslo is pretty average. Food is very basic, cold snacks / sandwiches, nothing out of the ordinary, probably the most basic of any lounge I have been in. WIFI is good. It's a shared lounge with pay to use customers. There is a segregated area behind the reception desk for business class customers. Nothing particularly bad, but nothing to get excited about either. Fair old walk to the departure gate (and you need to pass through passport control, which can get pretty busy) so leave plenty of time.

The international terminal has a lounge that is used by all airlines. It is well-signposted and is reached up a flight of stairs or an elevator after clearing security. With only one flight scheduled in the next five hours, there were only a few patrons. A selection of mostly Indian spirits were available at the bar, served by an attendant. TV screens showed a National Geographic animal program. Cold soft drinks, water, and beer were available. Nearly all of the food were Indian-style and looked unappetizing. One computer available for use, free wifi - decent speed. A collection of newspapers and trade magazines. Very comfortable couches to rest. Good place to relax before a flight. There is a dedicated restroom, including shower facilities, for the patrons and from the balcony one can look down at the departure concourse -- at that hour it was empty save for a couple of armed soldiers patrolling. Overall, it is a basic, serves-the-purpose lounge.

The Al Maha Transit Lounge was full but had sufficient seats available when I was there. There were two types of seats to choose from; armchair seats with low tables and those for dining cafeteria style. There were plenty of staff on hand to ensure that plates and glasses are quickly cleared and the tables cleaned. The food was delicious, fresh and there is a decent variety of choices from soups, salads, hummus, breads, sandwiches, desserts and a coffee machine. There was a chef on hand who replenished the food regularly which is nice. The toilets were packed so for those wishing to have a shower, they might have to wait for sometime.

Qatar Airways use the DNATA Lounge at Singapore Changi Airport which is very nice, it has very nice showers, it features a reading area, a big selection of food choices. The lounge is not big, which is good. The WiFi connection was perfect. It is a little bit far away from the gates.

column7: best lounge I have been to
column10: The Qatar Airways Al Mourjan Business Class Lounge in Doha is the best lounge I have been to. There are two restaurants and couple bars, a cigar lounge, a business conference area and wide choice of soft and alcoholic drinks. The people that work there were extremely friendly and nice.

column7: food of a very high standard
column10: The Qatar Airways lounge was pretty empty on both visits, food was of a very high standard along with excellent showers. Plenty of space to relax. Only issue was very slow internet, as it is free across the airport. Plenty of staff around to assist you. Very easy access from main concourse.
